data.title: Biofrontera signals 2025 record revenue ambitions with new Ameluz patent and cost controls
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Biofrontera Inc. (BFRI) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Hermann Luebbert opened with, "Our first quarter was a busy and exciting period for us. We continued our revenue growth while keeping our costs under control. Total revenues for the first quarter of 2025 were $8.6 million, a 9% increase from the same period of the prior year." He emphasized, "We strongly believe our past investments, execution and tremendous efforts to increase the effectiveness of our sales force will allow us to achieve record revenues in 2025 without increasing our costs."
  • Luebbert highlighted the granting of a new patent on the reformulated Ameluz, noting, "This new formulation, which lacks the potential allergen propylene glycol, has already been approved by the FDA and is in use since last year. Having a patent protection on this Ameluz formulation until December 2043, gives us another 18.5 years of protection from generic competition."
  • He described clinical development milestones, including the enrollment of the final patient in a Phase III trial for Ameluz in actinic keratoses on the extremities, neck, and trunk. The company is also pursuing FDA approval for label extension and for Ameluz in superficial basal cell carcinoma and acne.
  • Luebbert stated, “We expect to submit the new data to the FDA in the second half of this year. Following the approvals for AK on the entire body and for superficial basal cell carcinoma, we are aiming to -- at getting Ameluz approved for the treatment of moderate to severe acne.”
  • CFO Fred Leffler reported, "Total revenues for the first three months ended March 31, 2025, were $8.6 million, an increase of $0.7 million or 8.7% as compared to the three months ended March 31, 2024." Leffler detailed, "Cost of revenues, related party were $3.1 million for the first quarter of 2025 compared with $4.0 million for the prior year quarter. This decrease of $0.9 million or 22.1% compared to last year was due to the reduced cost structure under the last amendment of the Ameluz license and supply agreements."
  • Leffler highlighted, "The net loss for the first quarter of 2025 was $4.2 million or $0.47 per share, compared with a net loss of $10.4 million or $2.88 per share for the prior year quarter."

Outlook

  • Management expects to achieve record revenues in 2025, supported by increased sales force effectiveness and cost controls. Luebbert explained, “We believe we have built the foundation with the sales team and back end support to continue to improve our results on a consistent basis.”
  • The company plans to submit new data on Ameluz for label expansion to the FDA in the second half of 2025 and expects Phase II acne study data toward the end of the year.
  • No quantitative guidance or numerical projections for the coming quarters were disclosed in the transcript.

Financial Results

  • Revenues for Q1 2025 were $8.6 million, up $0.7 million year-over-year, primarily attributed to increases in Ameluz sales and the launch of the RhodoLED XL lamp.
  • Operating expenses fell to $13.1 million from $13.4 million the prior year quarter, with cost of revenues, related party down to $3.1 million from $4.0 million.
  • SG&A expenses decreased by $0.6 million, while research and development expenses increased by $1.2 million due to the assumption of all clinical trial activities for Ameluz in the US.
  • Net loss narrowed to $4.2 million, with improved gross profit and adjusted EBITDA, driven by cost reductions and sales growth.
  • Cash and cash equivalents were $1.8 million as of March 31, 2025, down from $5.9 million at the end of 2024.

Q&A

  • Jonathan Aschoff, ROTH Capital: "Over 1Q '25, how many lamp units did you sell, both the original and the XL?" Fred Leffler: "Placements as of Q1 were 18 -- we placed 18 of the XL lamps." Aschoff followed up about original lamp sales, to which Leffler replied he would need to double check the number.
  • Aschoff asked about sales force attrition and cost savings. Leffler responded, "We're working on bringing in some more -- what we call like a more junior rep... Some of that comes with a bit lower salary. And then some of it is -- has been some turnover, but we're committed to replacing that and reorganizing the territories and the team to be as efficient as possible to finish out the year strong."
  • Bruce Jackson, The Benchmark Company: Asked about the impact of transfer pricing changes on gross margins for the rest of the year. Leffler replied, "All of the inventory we have now is at the 25% transfer price, and that's what we will see for the rest of the year. It might be offset...based on how many lamps we sell because the margin there is lower."
  • Jackson also inquired about reimbursement status for the three tube indication. Luebbert said, "We are not aware of a single case where a doctor has been refused payment because of using more than one tube. So this seems to be completely solved."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral, focused on clarification of lamp sales, sales force structure, gross margin impacts, and reimbursement. Questions were direct without overt skepticism or enthusiasm.
  • Management maintained a confident and slightly positive tone, emphasizing strategic milestones, cost controls, and clinical progress. Luebbert highlighted, “We believe we have built the foundation...to continue to improve our results on a consistent basis.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment remained stable, with management reiterating confidence in achieving record results, and analysts maintaining a fact-finding approach.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 2025 revenue growth (9% year-over-year) was highlighted, following a record sales year in 2024.
  • Cost controls continued to be a focus, with reductions in cost of revenues and SG&A, while R&D expenses rose due to clinical trial activities.
  • Strategic initiatives remained consistent, with the new Ameluz patent and clinical trial milestones representing the main developments this quarter.
  • Management's confidence remained strong, with a continued emphasis on reaching breakeven and expanding Ameluz indications. Analysts' tone was unchanged, focusing on operational details and growth drivers.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted increased legal expenses related to patent claims, partially offset by savings elsewhere.
  • Cash and cash equivalents declined to $1.8 million, which may indicate a need for further capital or careful cash management.
  • The company is dependent on successful clinical trial outcomes and FDA approvals for key growth initiatives.

Final Takeaway

Biofrontera management underscored a quarter of progress in revenue growth, cost discipline, and product innovation, anchored by a new long-term patent for Ameluz and advancing clinical milestones. With an explicit expectation of record revenue for 2025 and ongoing label expansion efforts, the company continues to prioritize efficiency and market penetration while navigating legal and cash flow challenges. Investors are guided to watch for upcoming FDA submissions and data readouts as pivotal drivers for the remainder of the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: U.S., EU exchange negotiating papers as trade talks restart - report
data.description:
• Representatives from the U.S. and EU have restarted stalled trade talks as the two sides exchanged negotiating documents over the last few days.
  • The Financial Times reported that the documents deal with tariffs, digital trade, and investment opportunities, citing sources and an EU briefing note it saw.
  • That note included a message from Sabine Weyand, the European Commission's top trade official, who warned that the members states should not give into the U.S. preference for "quick wins," adding that tariffs in areas such as cars and steel are likely to stay.
  • U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer reportedly told European officials in private that the U.S. is getting fed up with the EU's inability to provide any proposals. Greer added the EU should brace for President Trump to reinstate tariffs introduced in April to their original levels.
  • The FT noted that EU Trade Commissioner Maroš Šefčovič spoke to Greer on Thursday and said he wanted to meet him in June at an OECD meeting in Paris.

data.title: Caterpillar upgraded to Hold from Sell at UBS as downside trade risk reduced
data.description:
Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT) +1.1% in Friday's trading as UBS upgraded shares to Hold from Sell with a $357 price target, raised from $272, as the bank no longer sees the same extent of downside risk to the company's earnings multiples following the better than expected progress and outcomes of trade discussions with China.

UBS analysts led by Steven Fisher think recent actions by the U.S. and foreign counterparts are 
driving towards a "contained downside scenario," with lingering uncertainty serving as somewhat of a drag on demand but the downside should be less.

Fisher forecast U.S. non-residential construction will re-accelerate to 4% from flattish in 2025, which likely will allow for Caterpillar's (NYSE:CAT) North America construction machinery sales to reaccelerate to 3%-4% growth in H2 2026 vs. declines in prior quarters, and now expects margins to decline 240 bps Y/Y in 2025 compared to a prior outlook for a 390 bps decline.

"We think the economy is now experiencing a slow rollover, and this will show in macro numbers in the next couple of months," Fisher wrote. "With the choppy data ahead and a big run in the stocks, we retain some caution on CAT. However, we think the more material downside sentiment is curtailed for now, and hold a more balanced view as policy gets finalized."

UBS still sees a risk of recession putting more pressure on capital goods demand, as well as a risk that sluggish demand makes it challenging for Caterpillar (CAT) to fully recover tariff costs, but at the same time, "there is potential for strong power gen demand, a resurgence of investment in Europe, and still healthy U.S. construction markets to drive earnings to a new peak in the next couple of years."

data.title: Optimism is fueling the stock market as S&P 500 flirts with 5% gains for the week – analyst
data.description:
U.S. stocks are on pace for a 5% gain this week for the S&P 500 (SP500) as of Friday afternoon, bringing the index up nearly 1%year-to-date.

Chris Hussey, managing director at Goldman Sachs, said that investors have been reacting to easing U.S. import tariffs and improved global GDP growth prospects, contributing to the market’s recent gains.

“The recent market performance demonstrates remarkable resilience in the face of earlier uncertainties,” he said.

A key factor supporting the recent rally has been another strong earnings season, Goldman Sachs analysts noted. First-quarter 2025S&P 500 (SP500) EPS are trending up 12% year-over-year, double the consensus forecast.

Also, following the relaxation of trade barriers, Goldman Sachs has raised its 2025 S&P 500 (SP500) EPS forecast to $262 from $253,highlighting corporate America’s continued profitability growth.

Foreign investment also appears poised to provide a lift to the U.S. economy, Hussey indicated. Analyst Joseph Briggs estimated an uplift to annual investments of $30-135B over the next few years from foreign announcements.

“These investment flows represent a significant vote of confidence in the U.S. economy,” said Hussey. He also suggested announced foreign purchases of U.S. goods could add another $50B annually to U.S. GDP if they materialize.

Regarding inflation, Goldman Sachs economists see little sign of an immediate tariff-fueled impulse, with their April PCE inflation forecast at a low 0.11% month-over-month. While inflation could still be a concern longer-term, a little inflation might help keep structural deflationary forces at bay, analysts said.

“The current inflation environment remains conducive to continued economic expansion,” Hussey added.

The prospect of a weaker U.S. dollar (DXY) is also considered a potential positive, according to Goldman Sachs analysts Kamakshya Trivedi and team.

They forecast a weaker dollar, which could add to any tariff-fueled inflation but potentially benefit U.S. exporters.

“Currency weakness often translates to competitive advantages for domestic manufacturers,” Hussey said, noting that a weaker dollar can make U.S. goods less expensive abroad and inflate foreign earnings when translated back into domestic currency.

data.title: Hinge Health IPO attracts more orders for shares than available: report
data.description:
As Hinge Health (HNGE) readies its initial public offering, the investors have flocked to own the shares of the digital health company, Bloomberg News reported Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.  

The demand for Hinge Health (HNGE) shares has exceeded the number of shares the company has made available for the IPO, the people said, requesting anonymity as the information is not public.

The San Francisco, California-based health tech started its IPO roadshow on Tuesday as it seeks to list its class A shares on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol 'HNGE.'

According to a filing this week, the company planned to raise more than $437M in proceeds by issuing 13.7M shares at $28-$32 apiece, targeting a valuation of up to $2.98B.

data.title: Surgery Partners gains amid report of AMSURG sale process
data.description:
Surgery Partners (SRGY) rose for a second day, gaining 1.3%, after a report that competitor AMSURG is in advanced stages of a sale process.

AMSURG is reportedly in a sales process that includes Tenet Healthcare's (THC) USPI,  UnitedHealth's (UNH) Surgical Care Affiliates as well Ascension Health, a private healthcare system, according to a DealReporter item on Thursday, UBS analyst AJ Rice wrote in a note on Friday. 

The AMSURG sales process could have implications for Surgery Partners (SRGY), which received a bid for $25.75 a share from Bain Capital in late January.  The bid came after Bloomberg reported in August that TPG Inc. (TPG) and UnitedHealth (UNH) were among suitors interested in the healthcare services firm. Bloomberg first reported in July that SGRY was exploring options, including a potential sale.

"A potential sale of AMSURG could also have implications for the valuation of Surgery Partners,"  Rice, who has a buy rating and $34 a share price target on SRGY, wrote in the note. "We think an AMSURG sale in the 8-10x multiple range could provide additional support to Bain's bid for SGRY (~10x multiple), while a sale of AMSURG at a mid-teens multiple could lead SGRY's board and shareholders to negotiate a higher sale price."

data.title: Analysts stay bullish and maintain optimism for Take-Two's bookings outlook
data.description:
Analysts continued to stay bullish on video game publisher Take-Two (NASDAQ:TTWO) following its fourth-quarter results, which were driven by continued strength in titles like NBA2K, Grand Theft Auto Online, and Red Dead Redemption 2. 

In Q4, bookings were $1.58B and non-GAAP EPS was $1.08, which is comparable to the consensus estimate of $1.55B and $1.10 per share, respectively.

The company recorded a goodwill impairment charge of $3.55B related to goodwill and $176.3M for acquisition-related intangible assets in the quarter.

For FY26, TTWO guided net bookings between $5.9B and $6.0B (estimate $7.8B). It also said Grand Theft Auto VI will help it achieve record levels of net bookings that will establish a new baseline for business and set the company on a path of enhanced profitability in FY27.

Research firm Oppenheimer (rating "outperform" PT $215) believes FY26 bookings guidance is in line with buyside and seems to have more conservative assumptions baked in, but noted that fundamentals for the company and sentiment continue to lean favorably for the stock.

"FY26 will have two immersive core games (Mafia, Borderlands 4) alongside two annual sports titles. FY27 and FY28 will have 12 immersive core games, excluding five sports titles and GTA 6. The higher counts of immersive core in FY27 and FY28 will likely support the new baseline beyond contribution from GTA 6," Oppenheimer said Friday.

Bank of America (rating "buy" PT $250) said a beat & raise cycle in FY26 should inspire investor confidence by showing that the base portfolio has room to grow, particularly if NBA 2K’s momentum continues.

BofA also believes investors will likely anchor their valuation on FY27 estimates given the release of GTA 6 will drive significant inflection.

J.P. Morgan (rating "overweight" PT $250) said the current outlook should not be compared to "stale Street estimates," which had yet to reflect the recently announced shift of GTA 6 from FY26 to FY27.

They noted that TTWO's bookings guidance assumes recurrent consumer spending to be flat year-on-year, with NBA expected to grow HSD percentage, offset by a decline in GTA Online and mobile.

data.title: Uber rallies to a new all-time high after its product event drives in more buyers
data.description:
Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) held its annual GO-GET event during the week to showcase its flagship products and unveil new features and services designed to make everyday life more affordable and convenient for users. Most reviews of the event have been favorable.

The event focused heavily on affordability, launching new ride options like Route Share, which was described as Uber's (NYSE:UBER) most economical ride yet, offering pickups every 20 minutes along busy commuter corridors at up to 50% less than UberX. The event also introduced price-lock and prepaid ride passes, allowing users to secure predictable fares or buy rides in bundles for additional savings. 

For the delivery part of the business, Uber Eats introduced the Savings Slider, enabling proactive price comparisons and substitutions to help users save on groceries. Uber One membership benefits were expanded, offering credits for Uber Rent and Lime rides, and the launch of Uber One Member Days was tipped off to include a week of exclusive deals for subscribers.

Goldman Sachs analyst Eric Sheridan said a significant highlight was Uber's (UBER) partnership with Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY) (OTCPK:VLKAF) to deploy fully electric, autonomous vehicles in Los Angeles by early 2026, marking a major step toward shared autonomous rides. Sheridan said the firm views the initiatives as consistent with Uber’s broader strategy to drive adoption, frequency, and retention by addressing key user pain points. 

Following the event, Goldman Sachs maintained a Buy rating on Uber (UBER) and its status as a Top Pick following the event. The firm's conviction is based on Uber's (UBER) ability to scale its end markets, improve profitability, and demonstrate powerful cross-sell and flywheel effects.

Shares of Uber Technologies (UBER) rose 2.1% in late Friday trading and rallied more than 10% for the week. The mobility tech stock carved out a new all-time high of $92.89 earlier in the session.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Coinbase jumps to the S&P 500, Google I/O event, and Computex
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - May 19

  • Volatility watch - Tesla (TSLA) and GameStop (GME) have a higher level of implied volatility than normal based on options trading. Companies with an elevated level of short interest outstanding on them include Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) and Sana Biotechnology (SANA). The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Regulus Therapeutics (RGLS), Dave (DAVE), and BioSig Tech (BSGM). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include UnitedHealth (UNH), B&G Foods (BGS), and Myriad Genetics (MYGN).
  • COMPUTEX - COMPUTEX 2025 with its AI Next theme will be a major focus of the tech sector. Over the four-day conference, NVIDIA (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang and Qualcomm (QCOM) CEO Cristiano Amon will deliver keynotes. Other presentations are scheduled from leaders of Arm Holdings (ARM), Intel (INTC), Adobe (ADBE), Cadence Design (CDNS), Infineon (OTCQX:IFNNY), Seagate Technology (STX), Compal (OTCPK:CMPCY), and NXP Semiconductors (NXPI).
  • Index developments - Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) will be added to the S&P 500 Index before the start of trading. Analysts have pointed to the development as a milestone for the crypto industry in general. Shopify (SHOP) will become a component of the Nasdaq 100 at the beginning of trading.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Trip.com (TCOM) and Agilysys (AGYS).
  • Fed watch - Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic will moderate a discussion before the 2025 Financial Markets Conference hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta. New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will participate in a moderated discussion on the economic outlook before the Mortgage Bankers Association Secondary and Capital Markets Conference.
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Chevron (CVX), Alcoa (AA), Hasbro (HAS), and Discover (DFS).
  • IPO watch - Digital health company Hinge Health (HNGE) and advertising software company MNTN (MNTN) are expected to price their IPOs and begin trading during the week.
  • All day - Microsoft (MSFT) will hold its annual Microsoft Build conference. CEO Satya Nadella will give the opening keynote on Microsoft's vision and AI strategy. Analysts think the deep dives into Microsoft Copilot, new AI integrations across Windows, Azure, and Microsoft 365, and practical demos of AI-driven development tools could perk up interest in the stock.
  • All day - JPMorgan Chase (JPM) will hold its annual Investor Day. Senior management is expected to provide updates on the company's strategy, financial outlook, and business segments.
  • All day - The four-day Dell Technologies World event will begin. CEO Michael Dell will be one of the featured speakers.

 

Tuesday - May 20

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Home Depot (HD), Palo Alto Networks (PANW), and Amer Sports (AS). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Pony AI (PONY) and Viking Holdings (VIK) after their reports are released.
  • Fed watch - Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin will speak on growth in rural communities before a conference hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond. St. Louis Federal Reserve Bank President Alberto Musalem will speak on the U.S. economy and monetary policy in a moderated conversation before the Economic Club of Minnesota. San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly and Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack give keynotes, and Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic moderates a discussion before the 2025 Financial Markets Conference.
  • All day - Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) will hold its annual Google I/O Conference. AI, and specifically Google's Gemini family of models, are expected to dominate the agenda. CEO Sundar Pichai will deliver the main keynote and unveil major product and AI updates.
  • All day - Notable investor events include nCino (NCNO) hosting its Investor Day, Diageo (DEO) hosting its annual Guinness Investor and Analyst Event, and Light & Wonder's (LNW) Investor Day.
  • All day - WTI crude June futures will expire. Crude oil futures have seen extra volatility over the last year on contract expiration dates.
  • 9:00 a.m. Home Depot (HD) will hold its earnings conference call. Major suppliers to Home Depot (HD) that have a high trading correlation to the home improvement stock on earnings day include Masco (MAS), American Woodmark (AMWD), and Central Garden & Pet (CENT). Options trading on Home Depot (HD) implies a 5% share price move after the report is released.
  • 2:00 p.m. Whirlpool Corporation (WHR) will participate in the J.P. Morgan 18th Annual Homebuilding & Building Products Conference.

 

Wednesday - May 21

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Target (TGT), TJX Companies (TJX), Lowe's (LOW), Snowflake (SNOW), Baidu (BIDU), XPeng (XPEV), and Zoom Communications (ZM). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for WeRide (WRD) and Urban Outfitters (URBN) after their reports are released.
  • All day - Chemed Corporation (CHE), Xenon Pharmaceuticals (XENE), CorMedix (CRMD), and Mind Medicine (MNMD) are scheduled to present at the RBC Global Healthcare Conference.
  • All day - Shareholders of Berkshire Hills Bancorp (BHLB) and Brookline Bancorp (BRKL) will vote on the planned $1.1 billion merger deal.

 

Thursday - May 22

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Intuit (INTU), Analog Devices (ADI), Workday (WDAY), Autodesk (ADSK), Ross Stores (ROST), Ralph Lauren (RL), and Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Webull (BULL) and Advance Auto Parts (AAP) after their reports are released.
  • All day - Embecta Corp. (EMBC) will hold an Analyst and Investor Day.
  • All day - Seagate Technology (STX) will hold an Analyst Day event.
  • 7:30 a.m. Fastenal (FAST) will begin trading on its split-adjusted basis following the 2-for-1 stock split.
  • 10:00 a.m. The Existing Home Sales report will be released by the National Association of Realtors.
  • 10:00 a.m. New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will give a keynote before a Monetary Policy Implementation Workshop hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

 

Friday - May 23

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Booz Allen (BAH).
  • All day - FTSE Russell will publish the preliminary lists of companies that are slated to be added to or removed from the Russell indexes, based on their market capitalizations and other eligibility criteria. Stocks slated for addition often see increased demand, while those facing deletion may experience selling pressure.
  • 10:00 a.m. The New Home Sales report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau.

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story.
